title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
fibroblasts in
fetal skin express more genes relating to Fb2 adult fibroblasts, including COL1A1, COL1A2
and COL6A1, suggesting they are functionally specialized towards ECM remodeling and
maintenance

Fb2 fibroblasts are significantly enriched compared to healthy skin and have upregulated
expression of the chemokines CXCL12 and CCL19
skin fibroblast (F2) cell program is also augmented in AD and psoriasis
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) define Fb2 (F2) as a disease-enriched fibroblast state upregulating CXCL12 and CCL19 in AD and psoriasis. Fetal skin fibroblasts share Fb2 gene expression including COL1A1, COL1A2 and COL6A1, suggesting ECM remodeling specialisation.

title: Single-cell analysis of psoriasis resolution demonstrates an inflammatory fibroblast state targeted by IL-23 blockade
quotes: vascular endothelial cells formed five sub-clusters (Fig. 4A, Supplementary Fig. 4A, B). These included arterioles (HEY1+/ S100A4+), capillaries (PLVAP+/RGCC+; ACKR1+/FABP4+), post-capillary venules (ACKR1+/SELE+) and venules (ICAM1+/VWF+)
summary: Francis et al. (2024) provide orthogonal evidence for fibroblast heterogeneity in psoriasis skin, consistent with the Reynolds Fb2 inflammatory fibroblast expansion. They also independently characterise post-capillary venules as ACKR1+/SELE+ in psoriasis skin.
